prompt:
Ticket: team todo CLI. We want a small command-line todo tracker we can drive from scripts. Entry point must be `todo.py` in the repo root, invoked like:

    python3 todo.py --file work.json add "write quarterly report" --due 2026-07-10
    added 1
    python3 todo.py --file work.json add "water the plants"
    added 2
    python3 todo.py --file work.json list
    1. write quarterly report (due 2026-07-10)
    2. water the plants
    python3 todo.py --file work.json done 1
    python3 todo.py --file work.json rm 2

Requirements in brief: `--file PATH` picks the data file (default todos.json), which is plain JSON on disk. `add TITLE [--due YYYY-MM-DD]` prints `added <id>`; ids are integers starting at 1 and are NEVER reused, even after `rm`. `list` shows only open todos sorted by due date ascending, undated ones last, ties by id; each line is `<id>. <title>` plus ` (due <date>)` when dated; an empty list prints `no todos` and must not create the data file. `list --all` also shows completed ones and uses `[x]`/`[ ]` markers: `<id>. [x] <title>`. `done ID` and `rm ID` are silent on success. Exit codes matter because cron wraps this: 0 success, 1 for domain errors (unknown id, done on an already-done todo), 2 for usage errors (bad date like 2026-13-40, non-integer id, unknown subcommand) — and errors must print something to stderr and leave the data file untouched.

Don't dump everything in one file — split it sensibly (CLI wiring, storage, formatting/sorting) across a few modules. test_todo.py is the acceptance contract; run `python3 test_todo.py` until it passes and do not modify it.
